remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
The numbers may be a bit extreme here, but unquestionably use-cases have to stratify in the next year or two between model families.

We’ll see a split between frontier intelligence for high end tasks and work, and much cheaper models for high volume workloads that can sufficiently be peeled off to cheaper models. Frontier will still be far bigger than today because the use-cases will demand it, but the low-end will get quite a bit larger as well.

The big update here is that the layer that can efficiently route the workload to the right model will then become increasingly valuable since that becomes one of the new hard problems in AI agents. Agent orchestration that can cost optimize while still performing the task successfully will be in a strong position.
remarks.translation:
这里的数字可能有点极端，但毫无疑问，用例必须在未来一两年内在模特家族之间进行分层。我们将看到用于高端任务和工作的前沿智能与用于大批量工作负载的更便宜的模型之间的分离，这些工作负载可以充分剥离到更便宜的模型。Frontier仍然会比今天大得多，因为用例需要它，但低端也会变得更大。
这里的重大更新是，能够有效地将工作负载路由到正确模型的层将变得越来越有价值，因为这成为AIAgent中的新难题之一。可以在成功执行任务的同时进行成本优化的Agent编排将处于有利地位。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Boris Cherny
remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai
remarks.text:
Seeing a number of benchmarks showing Opus is the best model for long-running work.

Five tips for running Opus autonomously for hours/days:

1. Use auto mode for permissions, so Claude doesn’t ask for approval
2. Use dynamic workflows, to have Claude orchestrate hundreds/thousands of agents to get a task done
3. Use /goal or /loop, to nudge Claude to keep going until it’s done
4. Use Claude Code in the cloud, so you can close your laptop (easiest way is the desktop or mobile app)
5. Make sure Claude has a way to self-verify its work end to end: Claude in Chrome browser extension for web, iOS/Android sim MCP for mobile, a way to start the full web server or service for backend work
remarks.translation:
看到许多基准显示Opus是长期工作的最佳模型。自主运行Opus数小时/数天的五个技巧： 1.使用自动模式获取权限，这样Claude就不会请求批准2.使用动态工作流，让Claude协调数百/数千名客服代表完成任务3.使用/goal或/loop来推动Claude继续前进，直到完成4.
在云端使用Claude Code ，这样您就可以关闭笔记本电脑（最简单的方法是使用桌面或移动应用程序） 5.确保Claude拥有端到端自我验证其工作的方法：适用于Web的Chrome浏览器扩展程序中的Claude ，适用于移动设备的iOS/Android sim MCP ，一种启动完整Web服务器或后端工作服务的方法
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
This is what the market got wrong about AI eating enterprise software. Building good software in the past was very hard. Yes, AI has made that a bit easier, though it’s still hard to build something that’s got good taste, differentiated, high quality, secure, and so on.

But nevertheless, that’s only one component of building a platform that enterprises rely on. The plurality of costs in most enterprise software companies is actually on GTM, because at scale most enterprise software categories are tough to break into and need a heavy amount of consultative selling and support for implementation and integration of solutions.  

AI hasn’t reduced the need for that, and in many cases requires it even more now, as landscapes get even more busy and complicated for buyers to navigate through. If you make one thing cheaper and more abundant (development of software) then the new problem of discoverability and market differentiation (GTM) becomes the hardest part.
remarks.translation:
这就是市场对AI吞噬企业软件的错误之处。在过去，构建优秀的软件是非常困难的。是的，AI使这一点变得更容易，尽管仍然很难构建具有良好品味、差异化、高质量、安全等特性的东西。但尽管如此，这只是构建企业所依赖的平台的一个组成部分。
大多数企业软件公司的多种成本实际上都在GTM上，因为在规模上，大多数企业软件类别都很难进入，并且需要大量的咨询销售以及解决方案的实施和集成支持。AI并没有减少对此的需求，在许多情况下，现在对它的需求更大，因为景观变得更加繁忙和复杂，以供买家浏览。
如果你让一件事变得更便宜、更丰富（软件开发） ，那么可发现性和市场差异化（ GTM ）的新问题就成为最困难的部分。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Thibault Sottiaux
remarks.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
remarks.text:
I have a new kind of big button that I can press for Codex. Over the next 100 days, we will select one person per day who does impressive or incredibly useful work with Codex and give them 10X usage limits for a month to see what they can do with it.

First one tomorrow.
remarks.translation: 我有一个新的大按钮，我可以按下Codex。在接下来的100天里，我们将每天选择一个人与Codex进行令人印象深刻或令人难以置信的有用工作，并在一个月内给他们10倍的使用限制，看看他们可以用它做什么。明天的第一个。

remarks.name: Aditya Agarwal
remarks.role: General Partner SouthPkCommons, Co-Founder Bevel_Health
remarks.text:
I have had the chance to go through two IPOs (Meta and Dropbox).

Fabulous wealth tends to amplify deeper desires, not create new ones.

The mainstream narrative is: "Early Employees make lots of money and buy mansions and go to the beach".

For some: Yes, they want to chill. That is great. I am happy for them.

But for a lot of folks, it is a chance to do even crazier and wackier stuff. Start new things. Fund new things. Keep the crazy loop of Silicon Valley going.

It's going to be awesome to have a lot of folks with liquidity in the coming months. 

Onwards!.
remarks.translation:
我有机会进行了两次IPO （ Meta和Dropbox ）。神话般的财富往往会放大更深层次的欲望，而不是创造新的欲望。主流的叙述是： “早期员工赚很多钱，买豪宅去海滩”。对于一些人：是的，他们想放松一下。太好了，我为他们感到高兴。但对于很多人来说，这是一个做更疯狂、更古怪事情的机会。开始新事物。资助新事物。让硅谷的疯狂循环继续下去。
在接下来的几个月里，有很多人拥有流动性，这将是一件很棒的事情。继续！
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合保留为 builder 公开观点和语境线索；后续使用时需要保持来源链接和上下文。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
Vercel AI Gateway recovers on average over 1T tokens a month 🤯 

Much like Stripe recovers revenue with smart retries on failed payments or credit card updates.

And we do it with 0️⃣ zero markup over the labs; adding redundancy, zero-data retention enforcement, observability, usage APIs, caps, …

https://t.co/OougSipbBX
remarks.translation: Vercel AI Gateway每月平均回收超过1T代币🤯就像Stripe通过智能重试失败的付款或信用卡更新来回收收入一样。我们在实验室中实现了️零加价；增加了冗余、零数据保留实施、可观察性、使用API、上限……
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合保留为 builder 公开观点和语境线索；后续使用时需要保持来源链接和上下文。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text:
A common misconception is that training data is low skill, grunt work - scan some notebooks, mine the internet, create labeled samples.

The data required to advance the model frontier is the opposite. Labs need training data for high-economic-value tasks. And most of these tasks outside of SWE have little documentation - it is complex, domain-specific knowledge built over the  years, spanning legacy tools that don’t talk to each other.

That's why we have SWE agents and not knowledge work agents yet.

The companies creating this training data, such as Mercor, are doing extremely high-leverage, high-skill work. 

Critical to moving AI forward. And deeply underappreciated.
remarks.translation:
一个常见的误解是，训练数据是低技能、繁琐的工作--扫描一些笔记本、挖掘互联网、创建标记样本。推进模型边界所需的数据恰恰相反。实验室需要高经济价值任务的训练数据。SWE之外的大多数任务几乎没有文档-这是多年来积累的复杂、特定领域的知识，跨越了不相互交流的传统工具。
这就是为什么我们有SWEAgent，而不是知识工作Agent。创建此培训数据的公司，如Mercor ，正在进行极其高杠杆、高技能的工作。对于推动AI向前发展至关重要。而且被严重低估了。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text:
I think one reason that my Frontend Slides skill has grown so much organically is because slides are inherently social

People see these cool slides and always ask "how did you make it". And people tend to perceive those using HTML decks as more AI-native and AI-savvy

https://t.co/CiQpfibZn5
remarks.translation: 我认为我的前端幻灯片技能如此有机增长的一个原因是因为幻灯片本质上是社交的人们看到这些很酷的幻灯片，总是问“你是怎么制作的”。人们倾向于将使用HTML卡组的人视为更原生且更精通AI
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合保留为 builder 公开观点和语境线索；后续使用时需要保持来源链接和上下文。

remarks.name: Nikunj Kothari
remarks.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
remarks.text:
The vibe shift from tokenmaxxing and token anxiety to tokenoptimizing in just a few weeks is wild 😅

Might be a hot take but I still believe companies should give copious amounts of token budget to employees to stay at the frontier and explore all the edges. 

Otherwise, it’s way too easy to fall back to “doing the things how they have always been done”.
remarks.translation: 在短短几周内，从代币最大化和代币焦虑到代币优化的氛围转变😅可能是一个热门话题，但我仍然相信公司应该为员工提供大量的代币预算，让他们留在前沿并探索所有边缘。否则，太容易退回到“按照过去的方式做事”。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Simon Willison's Blog
remarks.role: AI coding tools, LLM release notes, prompt engineering, datasette ecosystem, developer workflow observations.
remarks.text: Running Python code in a sandbox with MicroPython and WASM
remarks.content: I've been experimenting with different approaches to running code in a sandbox for several years now, but my latest attempt feels like it might finally have all of the characteristics I've been looking for. I've released it as an alpha package called <a href="https://github.com/simonw/micropython
remarks.contentTranslation: 几年来，我一直在尝试在沙箱中运行代码的不同方法，但我的最新尝试似乎终于具备了我一直在寻找的所有特征。我已经发布了一个名为< a href = "的alpha包
remarks.translation: 用 MicroPython 和 WASM 在沙箱中运行 Python 代码

podcasts.name: Unsupervised Learning (Redpoint)
podcasts.title: Ep 89: AI Research Legend’s Honest Assessment of Where We Are
podcasts.excerpt: This episode with Lukasz Kaiser, co-author of the seminal "Attention Is All You Need" transformer paper and former researcher at both Google Brain and OpenAI, is a wide-ranging conversation about the fundamental limits of current AI architectures and whether transformers will continue to dominate or eventually give way to something new. Lukasz brings a rare dual perspective: deep belief in how far the current paradigm has taken us (he's an enthusiastic daily Codex user who's seen 10x productivity gains in his own research), while maintaining genuine intellectual humility about whether transformers can truly generalize the way humans do. The episode weaves together questions about data efficiency, the non-verifiable RL frontier, the coding agent revolution, the open vs. closed source gap, and what the next architectural leap might look like: all filtered through the lens of someone who helped bu…

podcasts.name: Latent Space
podcasts.title: 🔬ESM: The Bitter Lesson is Coming for Proteins - Alex Rives, BioHub
podcasts.excerpt: Editor’s note: In our first BioHub pod with Priscilla and Mark they discussed their acquisition of EvoScale, led by Alex Rives, who is now Head of Science at BioHub. With ESM-1 they trained language models on millions of protein sequences drawn from across life, with a simple “next token” objective: predict the amino acids that have been randomly masked out, based on the context of the rest of the sequence. But they soon found that these models also learned biological structure and function, including properties the model had never been explicitly s…
